channel.title: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Finding the Scarlet King's children." - The SCP-231 phenomenon created 6 monsters, where are they now? (NSFW and disturbing sexual themes)

item.title: Re: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
Re: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
item.content:encoded: I like this train of thought. Most of those have preexisting origins, though obviously the foundation could just lie in the file if it were something as important as SCP-██. In any case, I like the idea that some or all of the kids are not big monsters like 682 but mostly normal humans. After all, their mother was still a █████, regardless of what Big Red was.

item.title: Re: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
item.content:encoded:
In the Serpent's Hand Hub, SCP-017 is called the Second Child which I assume is the second child of the Scarlet King. Also Summer's Exile which is about SCP-682 mentions the Fifth Child which suggests SCP-053, and my theory is the reason that those two get along is because they are siblings, or at least half-siblings, with SCP-682 being first generation and SCP-053 being second generation from the same father, the Scarlet King.

And then of course, there's the story about SCP-999 being the seventh child, which now accounts for three of the six surviving children, assuming the fourth is still dead. My guess is SCP-239 and SCP-040 are two others, and while I doubt it could work by context, I think it would be intesting to reveal SCP-105 is actually the first child of the Scarlet King and that Team Iris of Alpha-9 is critical in defeating him the next time he breaks free.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Detective YZ, instead of posting multiple comments in succession, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

item.title: Re: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
item.content:encoded:
I think 035 is made by Hanged King.
682 is the one we are most sure is the child of the Scarlet King(which makes him the one we most are not sure about..) 2030 could be a child of the Scarlet King, since he has no back story, like 1879.
447 is sentient? I mean, like 999? It is not really impossible since this is Scp universe we are talking about.
I think I need to think about 2774 and 4666.

Also, I like the idea of children of the Scarlet King is being called the Seven Deadly Sins, tho there are multiple children of Scarlet King from same brides, so I guess it doesn't exactly make sense.

item.title: Re: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
item.content:encoded:
Hmm.. Let me see; 682(maybe 4715, too?), 999, 106, 293, Dr. Clef, Dr. Montauk; the ones who are cursed/possessed by curses Scarlet King: 1504, 053, 049, 096, Connor(from Confinement series by Lord Bung, since there are multiple children of Scarlet King from same brides, it could be considered canon); there also are alot of other Scps that are not directly related to Scarlet King, mostly the overpowered ones without any known origin stories.
I also believe 049 was a child of Scarlet King, then forced to take side of Hanged King.

item.title: Re: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
item.content:encoded: I think I saw a draft for a Scarlet Prince SCP a while back, but I don't think it was any good. It could be a fun and emotional pyschological thriller if it was written well enough, though!

item.title: Re: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
item.content:encoded: I think you should rephrase that - none of the brides "enjoy" being raped, and being raped doesn't make you "evil".

item.title: Re: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
item.content:encoded: How does New Job conflict with the and Dust and Blood? Because both says that the child of the seventh bride isn't evil because she doesn't enjoy her being raped

item.title: Re: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
item.content:encoded: Removed. Don't advertise drafts on other people's work. - Staff

item.title: Re: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
item.content:encoded: My headcanon is that no SCPs at the moment are the children of the Scarlet King. I do have an article planned to be the child of the Scarlet King though.

item.title: Re: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
Re: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
item.content:encoded: They were all made in effigy of the original seven, weren't they? I feel like that was mentioned somewhere around here.

item.title: Re: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
item.content:encoded:
There's also the fact that there's no rule mentioning that there are only seven beings that will ever be the Seven Bride

Actually I think that's about as close to "canon" as one can get, considering the SC had at-least 14 brides, maybe even 21. Since SCP-231-1 through -7's parents were apparently also brides. Really it could go on even before them.

item.title: Re: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
item.content:encoded:
To make things even more convoluted, because that's what we're here for, let's consider that SCP-231 was an experiment by Children of the Scarlet King to imitate the Seven Brides—presumably A'tivik through A'habbat you've mentioned. It's possible 231 was actually wed by Scarlet King, but it's also entirely possible they're just infused with the King's essence, mutating the child they already have prior of the experiment.

There's also the fact that there's no rule mentioning that there are only seven beings that will ever be the Seven Bride: there's the possibility that unlike the King, the Brides are not immortal, and throughout his existence the King had sired many more children with many sets of Seven Brides.

item.title: Re: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
item.content:encoded:
682 is implied to be the child of the fourth bride. As for the others…

The first bride was A’tivik. She was beloved of the King, though her children were few. For her loyalty, her children were made wise above all others, and knew well the ways of war. By their hands, they guided the war, and lead to victory.

The second bride was A’ghor. A great hole was rent in her soul that she could not fill, and so she despaired and wept. She brought forth many children, and her children brought forth armies in a tide unthinking, to go forth and conquer.

The third bride was A’distat. She had a great hatred of her sisters and brought ruin upon all she surveyed, and blasphemed upon sacred ground. Her children ride out to declare the triumph of the King, drowning battlefields in blood and ash, spreading pestilence and fear in their wake.

The fourth bride was A’zieb. She was vast and powerful, and terrible to behold, taking the form of a great beast. Her children were like her, and feared no weapon nor magical spell, for their injuries were healed, and their hides impenetrable.

The fifth bride was A’nuht. She was strong in mind, though frail in body. Her children were wise in the ways of magic, and created great destruction. But because of their power, the King had them crippled, so that they might not rise up against him.

The sixth bride was A’tellif. She spoke not, and held herself private. Her children could change their faces and move about unseen, and walk among Creation unknown. They opened Ways between worlds, and made way for the war to spread.

The seventh bride was A’habbat. She was the smallest and weakest of the seven, but she was not broken utterly by the King, and was horrified by her state. Her children walked on two legs, and were mighty hunters and heroes: she taught them in secret, hoping that they might destroy the children of her sisters and overthrow the King. They are few, and they have failed.

There's a tale that says 999 is 231-7's kid, but that obviously conflicts with the aforementioned tale.

item.title: Finding the Scarlet King's children.
item.content:encoded:
According to SCP-231 each of the children are intended to give birth to somepart of a deity, with the seeming intent to undo Jesus Christ's teachings as a sort of "anti-Christ". Interestingly all of these children were designated with a 2-intiger SCP number, which doesn't exist. But maybe it did exist.

We know from SCP-233 (which not only effects pregnant humans but is also part of Clef's research) that the 3/4-digit system isn't how the foundation had always operated and from the twistedgears/kaktus proposal that the Foundation used to classify "separate" objects in different slots. So in theory SCP-048 could have been shortened to SCP-48.

Basically I'm suggesting each of the products of SCP-231 are in some way the "7 deadly sins" with the intended purpose of causing an the third worst K-class Scenario (XK, total failure of human morality).

  • The Light Bringer aka Lucifer the Fallen Angel: SCP-2030, It's a rather unorthodox take but still holds the same core concepts.
  • SCP-231-1 (Lust): The most simplistic and ultimately least dangerous child, SCP-106 or as it was called SCP-16 is a powerful reality warping entity that only uses it's powers for weird semi-sexual desires with no regard for who or what is destroyed in the process.
  • SCP-231-2 (Wrath): SCP-682 another reality-warping entity able to change it's own structure to adapt to nearly anything it wants has seemingly lost all drive beyond "getting back" at humanity, possibly for Procedure 110-Montauk.
  • SCP-231-3 (Greed): SCP-035 is capable of giving those who wear it untold power and knowledge, but consumes their minds until they are nothing more than a flailing piece of meat for SCP-035 to use.
  • SCP-231-4 (Envy): Due to it's exposure to SCP-500 SCP-447 is effectively dead and a substance rather than a lifeform. However consider the term *green with envy*.
  • SCP-231-5 (Sloth): SCP-2774 is again an entity that consumes the mind, ultimately capable of wining when the infect give into the urge to give up and rest.
  • SCP-231-6 (Pride): SCP-4666 or as it's more commonly known as "Santa Claus" isn't an actual physical being as much as an idea. All the traditions and "themes" related to Christmas are in some way a projection of it with the exception of the color green. In 1971 the Foundation used advanced computer-science to create a counter-meme that goes along with SCP-4666's red. In the end it's the idea that humans can create a "better" Christmas around gifts and happiness rather than the celebration of the ideal human's birth.
  • SCP-231-7 (Gluttony): The entity is yet to be born but it can be assumed that along with it's 7 brothers it will bring the end of human morality and sapient thought.
